title: Technical Program Manager
required_skills: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Security, STEM, or Business/Management with 8+ years IT experience
Active Secret security clearance (or ability to obtain TS/SCI)
Active DoD 8140 IAT Level II certification (Security+ or higher)
Experience with NIST 800-53 security controls, STIG hardening, DISA requirements, RMF lifecycle
CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure-as-code, automated deployment practices
matched_skills: Microsoft Office proficiency: confirmed across multiple roles
Cross-functional stakeholder management and program coordination: HSBC and Hapag-Lloyd
JIRA experience (bonus skill partial): listed as tool in CV
missing_skills: U.S. Citizenship (candidate is Germany-based, not a US citizen — absolute disqualifier)
Active Secret security clearance (not obtainable without US citizenship)
DoD 8140 IAT certifications (Security+, CASP+, CISSP) — no security certifications
Azure cloud architecture (no cloud infrastructure experience)
NIST 800-53, STIG, DISA, RMF lifecycle (no cybersecurity compliance experience)

Lead phased program initiation, planning, execution, and monitoring
Manage teams implementing NIST 800-53 security controls and RMF lifecycle
Translate mission requirements into technical implementation roadmaps
Interface with government stakeholders to communicate system status and risks
Oversee program-related contract actions and financial tasks
recommended_emphasis: This role is entirely inappropriate — application is impossible
US Citizenship is an absolute requirement that cannot be met
Security clearance requirements further disqualify the candidate
The technical cybersecurity domain is completely outside the candidate's background
summary: Match score 6.3% — poor match. This role requires US Citizenship, active DoD security clearance, and deep cybersecurity/cloud infrastructure expertise. The candidate is Germany-based, not a US citizen, and has zero cybersecurity or cloud engineering background. This is an absolute disqualifier — application is impossible and should not be pursued.
